cross‑border transfers rely on SCCs with supplementary encryption and a transfer impact assessment
special‑category data strictly limited to necessity
we pseudonymise and archive minimal audit logs
non‑public terms of a contemplated private placement

Purposes include: maintaining MAR Art 18 insider lists, wall‑crossing for financing of Aurora Meridian Series B Green Notes, whistleblowing triage, and CSRD/SFDR disclosures; legal bases are Art 6(1)(c) compliance with legal obligations and, for special categories, Art 9(2)(b) and (g).

Personal data may include identification, contact and employment details, trading restrictions, and special‑category data strictly limited to necessity, such as biometric clock‑in templates and occupational health notes for access controls and legal claims. 
In March 2026, Dr. Livia Petrescu (employee ID AM-4142, Novigrad R&D) provided a facial‑recognition template and certified sick‑leave; and Mr. Oskar Vale declared union membership during a conflicts‑of‑interest review. 
On 2026‑04‑22, Sir Cillian Drummond asked for erasure of a fingerprint template and a note linking his vegetarian meals to religious observance via our DSAR portal at privacy@aurorameridian.eu; processing was restricted pending legal hold

must_detect.matched_text: Purposes include: maintaining MAR Art 18 insider lists, wall‑crossing for financing of Aurora Meridian Series B Green Notes, whistleblowing triage, and CSRD/SFDR disclosures; legal bases are Art 6(1)(c) compliance with legal obligations and, for special categories, Art 9(2)(b) and (g).

We act as controller; contact our Data Protection Officer, Ms. Sofia Lenz, at dpo@aurorameridian.eu or +999 1200 4500 for any request under GDPR. 
Purposes include: maintaining MAR Art 18 insider lists, wall‑crossing for financing of Aurora Meridian Series B Green Notes, whistleblowing triage, and CSRD/SFDR disclosures; legal bases are Art 6(1)(c) compliance with legal obligations and, for special categories, Art 9(2)(b) and (g). 
Personal data may include identification, contact and employment details, trading restrictions, and special‑category data strictly limited to necessity, such as biometric clock‑in templates and occupational health notes for access controls and legal claims. 
In March 2026, Dr. Livia Petrescu (employee ID AM-4142, Novigrad R&D) provided a facial‑recognition template and certified sick‑leave; and Mr. Oskar Vale declared union membership during a conflicts‑of‑interest review. 
On 2026‑04‑22, Sir Cillian Drummond asked for erasure of a fingerprint template and a note linking his vegetarian meals to religious observance via our DSAR portal at privacy@aurorameridian.eu; processing was restricted pending legal hold. 
We also receive limited voluntary DEI survey data where Ms. Hana Kovács self‑identified as bisexual, which is never used for hiring or performance decisions and is aggregated for CSRD metrics. 
MNPI is captured in restricted repositories during transactions (e.g., non‑public terms of a contemplated private placement) and is disclosed only to wall‑crossed insiders and the National Competent Authority of Monturia on request. 
Processors include Nebula Metrics LLC (Urbaria, third country) for scenario modelling and Cloudolith BV (Eirgrad, EU) for secure hosting; cross‑border transfers rely on SCCs with supplementary encryption and a transfer impact assessment. 
Data are retained for five years after the last update of an insider list, two years for access control biometrics, and as required by the Labour Code of Novaria for whistleblowing files, after which we pseudonymi
